commit.message:
Smarter artifact extraction: pick message with heading, not just last

Instead of blindly taking the last assistant message, collect all
messages and pick the longest one containing a markdown heading.
This handles Claude adding trailing commentary after the artifact.
Combined with preamble stripping, output is now reliably clean.

commit.message:
fix(security): Pass 11 batch — 13 fixes across 12 files

CMB11-001 CRITICAL: Fix friendly-fire molecule refund column name bug
  - game_actions.php: SELECT id,classe→SELECT id (classe column does not exist)

AUTH11-001 HIGH: Logout requires POST + CSRF to prevent CSRF-triggered logout
  - deconnexion.php: add POST check + csrfCheck() before session destruction
  - basicprivatehtml.php: change nav logout link to POST form with CSRF token

ALL11-001 HIGH: Prevent self-war declaration
ALL11-002 HIGH: Prevent self-pact proposal
ALL11-003 MEDIUM: Prevent chef self-transfer
  - allianceadmin.php: explicit tag/login === own alliance checks

RANK11-001/002/003 CRITICAL: Fix IP hashing consistency (hash→hash_hmac)
  - logger.php, csrf.php, basicpublicphp.php, inscription.php (×2),
    admin/index.php, moderation/index.php: SHA-256 concat → HMAC-SHA256

INFRA11-001 HIGH: getSpecModifier() static cache not invalidated after spec update
  - formulas.php: add __INVALIDATE__ sentinel to getSpecModifier()
  - player.php: invalidatePlayerCache() now also clears spec cache

ADMIN11-001 HIGH: Alliance VP award not idempotent on season retry
  - migrations/0099: add season_vp_awarded flag to alliances
  - player.php: Phase 1c VP award uses WHERE season_vp_awarded=0 guard
  - player.php: remiseAZero() resets season_vp_awarded=0

ADMIN11-002 HIGH: News XSS via HTML entity-encoded javascript: URIs
  - index.php: use preg_replace_callback with html_entity_decode before check

ECO11-005 MEDIUM: Depot downgrade doesn't clamp stored resources
  - player.php: diminuerBatiment() clamps all ressources to new placeDepot() on depot decrease

RANK11-007 HIGH: Espionage not monitored by anti-cheat coordination detection
  - attaquer.php: call checkCoordinatedAttacks() after successful espionage

重写智能洞察：从数据展示升级为异常检测型反直觉建议

- CTR 异常检测：排名好但 CTR 异常低 → 标题/描述问题
- 趋势检测：近7天 vs 前21天对比，发现加速/减速搜索词
- 未覆盖关键词集群：有曝光但无专门页面 → 内容缺口
- 排名-CTR 错配：首页排名但低 CTR → Snippet 优化
- 跳出率信号：GA4 高跳出率 → 内容意图不匹配
- 按影响力排序的行动建议（紧急/高/中/低优先级）

refactor(core,primary-node): add Primary Node type definitions (Issue #1040) (#1170)

This PR adds shared type definitions for Primary Node as the first step of
Issue #1040 (separating Primary Node code to @disclaude/primary-node).

## Changes

### @disclaude/core
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| packages/core/src/types/primary-node.ts | New file - Primary Node type definitions |
| packages/core/src/types/index.ts | Export Primary Node types |

### @disclaude/primary-node
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| packages/primary-node/src/index.ts | Re-export types from @disclaude/core |

## Types Added

- NodeType - Node type identifier ('primary' | 'worker')
- NodeCapabilities - Node capability flags (communication, execution)
- BaseNodeConfig - Base configuration for all node types
- PrimaryNodeConfig - Primary Node configuration
- RestChannelConfig - REST channel configuration
- FileStorageConfig - File storage configuration
- ExecNodeInfo - Information about connected execution nodes
- getNodeCapabilities() - Get capabilities for a node type

## Next Steps

This PR is a foundational change. Subsequent PRs will:
1. Move src/channels/ to @disclaude/primary-node
2. Move src/platforms/feishu/ to @disclaude/primary-node
3. Move src/ipc/ (server part) to @disclaude/primary-node
4. Move src/nodes/primary-node.ts and related files to @disclaude/primary-node

## Verification

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| All tests (1785) | Passed |
| TypeScript type-check | Passed |
| ESLint | 0 errors (only warnings) |

Closes #1040 (Phase 1)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat(multimodal): enhance image analyzer MCP prompt for better scheduling (Issue #656) (#1174)

## Summary

Enhanced the image attachment prompt in MessageBuilder to provide clearer guidance
for agents on how to analyze images using the image analyzer MCP tool.

### Changes

- Replaced conditional hint with mandatory analysis instruction
- Added detailed workflow: first analyze, then respond
- Provided explicit tool usage example with mcp__4_5v_mcp__analyze_image
- Added guidance for native multimodal vs MCP-based analysis
- Updated tests to reflect new enhanced prompt

### Before

```
**Note:** Image attachment(s) detected. If you need to analyze the image content...
```

### After

```
## 🖼️ Image Analysis Required
You MUST analyze the image content before responding...
[Detailed workflow and tool usage examples]
```

This ensures non-native multimodal models (like GLM-5) can effectively use
the image analyzer MCP with the same user experience as native multimodal models.

## Test Plan

- [x] All 14 tests in message-builder.test.ts pass
- [x] No new lint errors introduced
- [x] Manual verification of enhanced prompt content

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Implement Table, List, Tabs, and Overlay widgets (ADR-T27) (#24)

* feat: Implement Epic D dashboard widgets (Table, List, Tabs, Overlay)

Add four new widget types with full Rust native implementation, FFI bindings,
TypeScript wrappers, JSX reconciler support, and integration tests:

- Table: column definitions, row CRUD, cell get/set, selected row, header visibility
- List: item CRUD, selection, viewport offset (similar to Select but standalone)
- Tabs: label management, active tab navigation via LEFT/RIGHT keys
- Overlay: open/close, modal focus trapping, clear-under, dismiss-on-escape

Key changes:
- NodeType enum expanded (Table=6, List=7, Tabs=8, Overlay=9)
- State structs (TableState, ListState, TabsState, OverlayState) on TuiNode
- 27 new FFI functions following existing Select pattern
- Leaf node enforcement in tree.rs (Table/List/Tabs reject children)
- Keyboard event handlers for all four widget types
- Render implementations with viewport scrolling and selection highlighting
- TS widget classes, JSX prop interfaces, reconciler applyStaticProp cases
- 141 FFI tests passing, 49 JSX tests passing, 218 Rust tests passing

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* fix: Address PR review feedback for Epic D widgets

- Add missing tui_overlay_set_dismiss_on_escape / tui_overlay_get_dismiss_on_escape
  FFI functions with TS wrapper, JSX reconciler support, and tests
- Set OverlayState::dismiss_on_escape default to true (manual Default impl)
- Remove unused viewport_offset field from ListState
- Fix test symbol definition spacing for overlay section

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* feat: Implement modal focus trapping and clear_under rendering for Overlay

- Modal overlay traps Tab/BackTab focus within its subtree when modal=true
  and open=true, preventing focus from escaping to elements behind the overlay
- Mouse click focus changes are also blocked outside a modal overlay
- Closed overlays are skipped during focus traversal (children not reachable)
- clear_under rendering fills the overlay content area with background color
  before rendering children, erasing content rendered beneath
- Add integration test verifying modal focus trapping behavior

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* feat: Implement ESC key handling to dismiss overlay

When a focused node is inside an open overlay with dismiss_on_escape
enabled (default), pressing ESC closes the overlay and emits a change
event (data[0]=0). When dismiss_on_escape is false, ESC passes through
as a normal key event.

Add two Rust unit tests verifying both the dismiss and no-dismiss paths.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* fix: Guard against negative remaining width in column width calculation

Clamp `remaining` to 0 after subtracting fixed/percent column widths
in compute_column_widths(). Prevents negative values from propagating
when fixed-width columns exceed the total available width.

The table state cloning in render functions is acknowledged but matches
the existing Select widget pattern — both need clone to resolve the
borrow conflict between ctx.nodes (immutable) and ctx.front_buffer
(mutable via clip_set).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* style: Apply rustfmt formatting to overlay test assertions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* fix: Address code review issues for Epic D widgets

1. Add `rows` prop to TableProps JSX interface and handle it in the
   reconciler, matching the declarative pattern used by List (`items`)
   and Tabs (`tabs`).

2. Add missing overlay getters: `tui_overlay_get_modal` and
   `tui_overlay_get_clear_under` in Rust FFI, TS FFI bindings, and
   Overlay class methods (`isModal()`, `isClearUnder()`).

3. Replace `UnicodeWidthStr::width(ch.to_string().as_str())` with
   `UnicodeWidthChar::width(ch).unwrap_or(0)` in render.rs to avoid
   temporary String allocations per character in rendering loops.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

* docs: Update TechSpec to document all 8 Overlay FFI functions

Add tui_overlay_get_modal, tui_overlay_get_clear_under,
tui_overlay_set_dismiss_on_escape, and tui_overlay_get_dismiss_on_escape
to the Overlay Widget function table in §4.3.6. Update symbol count
from +4 to +8 for Overlay, and total from +38/134 to +42/138.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MypK1dcVvB1rZYjEyVwgYM

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve OOM error messaging to clarify memory limits are by design (#1714)

## Summary
Updated the out-of-memory (OOM) error message and related tests to
better communicate that memory limits are an intentional design feature,
not an error condition. The new messaging emphasizes that users should
retry with narrower queries rather than viewing this as a system
failure.

## Key Changes
- **Revised OOM hint message** to clarify that:
  - Memory limits are enforced by design and are normal/expected
  - This is NOT an error or bug
  - The solution is to retry with narrower, more filtered queries
- Explicitly instructs the agent not to alarm the user about operational
concerns
- Positions the `TOOL_MEMORY_LIMIT_MB` environment variable as an
optional fallback, not the primary solution

- **Updated test assertions** to:
- Verify the new messaging includes "NOT an error" to confirm the
clarification is present
- Simplified the assertion for memory limit display format from "current
limit: X" to "X MB"

## Implementation Details
The message now uses clearer language that distinguishes between:
1. **What happened**: Command exceeded memory limit (normal, by design)
2. **What to do**: Retry with filters, namespace/label selectors,
smaller time ranges
3. **Fallback option**: Increase limit via environment variable
(de-emphasized as secondary approach)

This change helps prevent user confusion and reduces unnecessary
escalations when memory limits are hit during normal operation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LQ5bAb71GzfvuGQFrU8scM

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Improvements**
* Improved error messaging for out-of-memory scenarios. When memory
limits are exceeded, users now receive a clearer message indicating this
is normal and expected behavior, accompanied by helpful recommendations
to retry using narrower queries or advanced filtering strategies to
optimize resource usage and successfully complete their intended
operations.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix: reset confirmedRef on edit session start to prevent lost blur saves

Root cause: confirmedRef.current was set to true when Enter was pressed to save a task name (to suppress the subsequent blur event from double-saving), but was never reset when a new edit session began. On the next edit, confirmedRef was still true from the previous session, so the blur handler would find it true, reset it, and return early — dropping the save entirely.

Fix: reset confirmedRef.current = false at every edit-session entry point (double-click, keypress, and auto-edit useEffect).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Comprehensive readability + UX enhancement pass

Text readability:
- Bump all font sizes: 7px→8.5px, 8px→9.5px, 8.5px→10px, 9px→10.5px,
  9.5px→11px, 10.5px→11.5px, 11.5px→12.5px, 6px→8px
- CSS classes: .tag 10→11px, .sl 12→13px, .hs-name 13→14px,
  .sub clamp min .85→.9rem

Performance:
- Aurora bands + orbs paused on inactive slides
- Particles reduced 50→35 (halves distance checks)
- Slide transition lock 800→650ms (snappier navigation)
- Animation stagger .d5-.d7 capped at .45-.55s (was .5-.7s)
- loading="lazy" on all 43 img tags

Accessibility:
- @media (prefers-reduced-motion) kills all animations
- Modal: role="dialog", aria-modal, aria-label
- Close button: aria-label="Close"
- Aurora divs: aria-hidden="true"
- Modal scrollbar visible (6px thumb)

Responsive:
- Added 900px and 768px tablet breakpoints
- Glass/stat sizing scales down for mobile
- h2/h3/sv/sv-sm fluid sizing at tablet widths

UX:
- Cover vignette re-enabled (radial gradient focus)
- Keyboard help hint shown on deck enter, fades after 5s
- Slide navigator overlay hint updated
